texto: We’ve entered dangerous territory. You can’t kiss someone with so much emotion if you’re preparing to walk away. Alarms ring through my head, too loud and too obvious to be ignored. There are way too many complex emotions being passed between us. I already know he’s going to shatter my heart.
explicacao: Citação de Jennifer Ann sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “We’ve entered dangerous territory. You can’t kiss someone with so much emotion if you’re preparin…” articula amor, love-story, na-romance em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como na-romance e new-adult-contemporary-romance.

frase_original: We’ve entered dangerous territory. You can’t kiss someone with so much emotion if you’re preparing to walk away. Alarms ring through my head, too loud and too obvious to be ignored. There are way too many complex emotions being passed between us. I already know he’s going to shatter my heart.

texto: Here’s the thing about falling for someone who’s already given up; there’s no promise of tomorrow. There aren’t any words of comfort that can be said, no glimpse of a positive change. Every moment, every thought could be their last. It’s like you’re helplessly walking into quicksand, waiting for the muck to cover your mouth and eyes until you can no longer find a way to breathe. No, it’s more like jumping from a high bridge without the promise of water underneath.And I fucking hate heights.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jennifer Ann: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Here’s the thing about falling for someone who’s already given up; there’s no promise of tomorrow…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, love-story, new-adult-contemporary pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e new-adult-contemporary.
